title: blepharoglossum foetulentum
side_effects: Blepharoglossum foetulentum can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing vomiting, diarrhea, and stomach cramps, which can lead to dehydration and electrolyte imbalances. Prolonged use or excessive consumption of the plant can also cause liver and kidney damage, as well as interactions with other medications. In rare cases, improper use of Blepharoglossum foetulentum can trigger allergic reactions, including anaphylaxis, and affect the cardiovascular system. It is essential to consult a medical professional before using this plant for medicinal purposes to minimize the risk of adverse side effects.
